ddls:
CREATE TABLE "epidemiological_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "country" TEXT /* Name of the country where the data is collected */,
  "cases" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed deaths */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was collected */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease being tracked */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Entity that reported the data */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Number of recovered cases */,
  "active_cases" INTEGER /* Number of active cases */,
  "data_version" INTEGER /* Version of the data entry */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the data has been verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_disease_name FOREIGN KEY ("disease_name") REFERENCES diseases ("disease_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., hospital, local health authority) */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diseases" (
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the disease */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, researcher) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each data entry
Name of the country where the data is collected
Region where the country is located
Timestamp of when the data was collected
Name of the disease being tracked
Indicates if the data has been verified
Unique identifier for each data source
Type of the data source (e.g., hospital, local health authority)
Contact information for the data source
Role of the user (e.g., admin, researcher)
